untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of 'foundational thesis' and 'expected direction' which are subjective trader beliefs, not objective price/indicator measurements. The core concept of thesis contradiction cannot be mechanically expressed without external trade-entry metadata (entry rationale). Provided best-effort proxy conditions based on first-bar rejection (small body, close opposite to open), but true signal intent requires knowledge of trader's original directional thesis at entry.
translation_notes: SCHWAGER_DRY_0_1 emphasizes exiting/reversing on day-1 contradiction of entry thesis. Approximated using: (1) small body rejection on day 1 (body_pct < 0.5%) as proxy for 'fails to sustain direction', and (2) close opposite to open as rejection signal. Exit triggers on subsequent close moving against the assumed direction. This assumes entry direction is known externally. Consider pairing with position metadata to improve accuracy.
